Module chariot
Package chariot.model

Interface Result<T>

All Known Implementing Classes:
Result.Fail, Result.Many, Result.One, Result.Zero
public sealed interface Result<T> permits Result.Zero<T>, Result.Fail<T>, Result.One<T>, Result.Many<T>
A "container" for responses, which can be of the types Result.One, Result.Many, Result.Zero or Result.Fail.

Examples of how to access a response value: 

     Client client = Client.basic();
     Result<User> result = client.users().byId("lichess");

     // 1: Not accessing the value directly, but printing the "raw" container (with value if inside)
     System.out.println(result.toString());

     // 2: Yolo get! Trying to access the value without knowing if it is present... (use with caution, might throw NoSuchElementException)!
     User user = result.get();

     // 3: Use if-statement to check if value is present
     if (result.isPresent()) {
         User user = result.get();
         System.out.println("Patron: " + user.patron());
     }

     // 4: Use a lambda if value is present
     result.ifPresent(user -> System.out.println("Patron: " + user.patron()));

     // 5: Use a lambda if value is present, or else another lambda
     result.ifPresentOrElse(user -> System.out.println("Patron: " + user.patron()), () -> System.out.println("Couldn't find user lichess!"));

     // 6: Stream the value
     result.stream().forEach(user -> System.out.println(user.id() + " Patron: " + user.patron()));

     // 7: instanceof pattern match
     if (result instanceof Result.One<User> one) {
         User user = one.entry();
         System.out.println("Patron: " + user.patron());
     } else if (result instanceof Result.Many<User> many) {
         Stream<User> users = many.entries();
         users.forEach(user -> System.out.println(user.id() + " Patron: " + user.patron()));
     }

     // 8: JEP 420: Pattern Matching for switch (Second Preview) / --enable-preview
     String message = switch(result) {
         case Result.One<User>  one  -> "Patron: " + one.entry().patron();
         case Result.Many<User> many -> many.entries().map(user -> user.id() + " Patron: " + user.patron()).collect(Collectors.joining("\n"));
         case Result.Zero<User> zero -> "No user found";
         case Result.Fail<User> fail -> fail.message();
     };
     System.out.println(message);

     // 9: Deconstruction patterns, "Future work" in JEP 420
     String message = switch(result) {
         case Result.One<User>(var user)     -> "Patron: " + user.patron();
         case Result.Many<User>(var users)   -> users.map(user -> user.id() + " Patron: " + user.patron()).collect(Collectors.joining("\n"));
         case Result.Zero<User>()        -> "No user found";
         case Result.Fail<User>(var message) -> message;
     };
     System.out.println(message);

    Failure
    static final record 
    Result.Many<T>

    One entry

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default String
    error()
    Returns a error message, in case the result is Fail.
    static <T> Result.Fail<T>
    fail(String message)
     
    default T
    get()
    Returns the entry.
    default T
    getOrElse(T t)
    Deprecated. 
    default void
    ifPresent(Consumer<T> consumer)
    If a value is present, performs the given action with the value, otherwise does nothing.
    If the result contains many entries, the action is performed on each entry See Optional.ifPresent(Consumer)
    default void
    ifPresentOrElse(Consumer<T> consumer, Runnable action)
    If a value is present, performs the given action with the value, otherwise performs the given empty-based action.
    If the result contains many entries, the action is performed on each entry See Optional.ifPresentOrElse(Consumer, Runnable)
    default boolean
    isPresent()
    If a value is present, returns `true`, otherwise `false`.
    See Optional.isPresent()
    static <T> Result<T>
    many(Stream<T> t)
     
    default <R> Result<R>
    map(Function<? super T,? extends R> mapper)
    If a value is present, applies the given function to the value.
    static <T> Result<T>
    one(T t)
     
    default T
    orElse(T t)
    If a value is present, it is returned.
    default Stream<T>
    stream()
    If a single value is present, returns a stream with that value.
    static <T> Result.Zero<T>
    zero()
     

  • Method Details

    • one

      static <T> Result<T> one(T t)

    • many

      static <T> Result<T> many(Stream<T> t)

    • zero

      static <T> Result.Zero<T> zero()

    • fail

      static <T> Result.Fail<T> fail(String message)

    • isPresent

      default boolean isPresent()
      If a value is present, returns `true`, otherwise `false`.
      See Optional.isPresent()

    • ifPresent

      default void ifPresent(Consumer<T> consumer)
      If a value is present, performs the given action with the value, otherwise does nothing.
      If the result contains many entries, the action is performed on each entry See Optional.ifPresent(Consumer)

    • ifPresentOrElse

      default void ifPresentOrElse(Consumer<T> consumer, Runnable action)
      If a value is present, performs the given action with the value, otherwise performs the given empty-based action.
      If the result contains many entries, the action is performed on each entry See Optional.ifPresentOrElse(Consumer, Runnable)

    • stream

      default Stream<T> stream()
      If a single value is present, returns a stream with that value. Or if there are multiple entries, returns those entries. See Optional.ifPresentOrElse(Consumer, Runnable)

    • get

      default T get()
      Returns the entry. Note, one should check if entry is present first.
      If there are multipe entries, the first entry is returned.
      If there is no entry available, throws NoSuchElementException.

    • getOrElse

      @Deprecated default T getOrElse(T t)
      Deprecated.
      Use orElse(Object) instead.

    • orElse

      default T orElse(T t)
      If a value is present, it is returned. If multiple values are present, the first value is returned. If no value is present, the provided value is returned. See Optional.orElse(Object)

    • map

      default <R> Result<R> map(Function<? super T,? extends R> mapper)
      If a value is present, applies the given function to the value. If multiple values are present, applies the given function to the first value. If no value is present, an empty result is returned. See Optional.map(Function)

    • error

      default String error()
      Returns a error message, in case the result is Fail.
